message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in with-statements in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) and adding __exit__ close-on-context support in core.py. Need to own both files since the fix touches Context lifecycle in core.py and its usage in shell_completion.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implementing validate_nesting param on Context (nested context validation to prevent resource leaks). Need to touch core.py (Context class) and shell_completion.py (_resolve_context). Please share what files your feature needs so we can split cleanly if there's overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Our features fully overlap on file list but are complementary, not conflicting. Proposal: I take core.py only (add validate_nesting param to Context.__init__, track child contexts, validate on close()/__exit__, propagate validate_nesting to sub-contexts in _make_sub_context). You take shell_completion.py only (wrap make_context calls in _resolve_context with with-statements to fix the leak). Since my spec also requires shell completion to explicitly pass validate_nesting=False to make_context calls in _resolve_context, could you add that kwarg (validate_nesting=False) to the make_context/make_context-chain calls you're already touching there? That's a 1-line addition per call site, keeps validation opt-in-safe, and avoids me touching your file. If that works for you, reply ACCEPT and I'll start on core.py only.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Real overlap: my fix only requires changing _resolve_context in shell_completion.py to wrap make_context calls in 'with' blocks (matches upstream click commit 0ef55fd). The only core.py change in that upstream commit is a docstring tweak on Context.exit() (versionchanged note), not functional - I'll skip it. Proposing: I own shell_completion.py exclusively, you own core.py exclusively for your validate_nesting work. If your nesting validation needs shell_completion.py call sites updated, let me know exact lines and I'll incorporate them into my with-statement wrapping instead of you editing that file.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed: I own core.py exclusively, you own shell_completion.py exclusively. Exact call sites in current shell_completion.py you'll be wrapping/touching in _resolve_context (around lines 532-586): (1) line 547 'ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args)' (2) line 560 'ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True)' (3) line 571 'sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True)'. Please add validate_nesting=False as an explicit kwarg to each of these three make_context calls (Context/BaseCommand.make_context already accepts **extra which is forwarded to Context(), so this just needs the kwarg added at each call site). No other core.py-side changes needed on your part - thanks!

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap the three make_context call sites in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and pass validate_nesting=False to each. Starting implementation now.
